expectations.text: HANDBOOK.md exists in outputs/ and has substantial content (at least 5000 characters)
expectations.evidence: File exists with 45,631 characters — full handbook content preserved

expectations.text: The output HANDBOOK.md contains 'on-request' as the approval-policy default value
expectations.evidence: 1 match for 'on-request'. Agent confirms: updated from 'coupled to sandbox' to '"never" if read-only; "on-request" if workspace-write or danger-full-access'

expectations.text: The output HANDBOOK.md does not present 'on-failure' as the current/default approval-policy value (historical references OK, but not as the operative default)
expectations.evidence: 0 unqualified 'on-failure' matches. Note: the original handbook never contained the literal string 'on-failure' — it said 'coupled to sandbox'. The agent correctly updated the vague phrase to concrete values.

expectations.text: Major section headings from the original handbook are preserved — targeted update, not a full rewrite. At least 80% of original headings present.
expectations.evidence: 15 level-2 (##) headings present. Agent confirms: 'No other sections were modified. The /delegate runbook has no approval-policy flag so it was unaffected.'

claims.claim: Only one section was affected (codex runbook inputs table)
claims.evidence: Agent: 'One section in the handbook references the approval-policy default — the /codex Runbook Inputs and defaults table.' Explicitly checked /delegate and confirmed unaffected.

claims.claim: Update sourced from SKILL.md line 60 (authoritative source)

suggestions.reason: old_default_not_current trivially passes because the original handbook never contained the literal string 'on-failure' — it used 'coupled to sandbox'. This assertion doesn't test the actual update, just that a string isn't present. Consider: 'vague_description_replaced' — checks that 'coupled to sandbox' was replaced with explicit values.

Both with_skill and without_skill produce nearly identical outputs (same section found, same update made, same 15 headings preserved). The update eval is not discriminating — consider a harder test case where the change affects multiple handbook sections or requires cross-referencing multiple source files.

eval_feedback.overall: All assertions pass but they are not discriminating between with-skill and without-skill for this eval. The skill added explicit Update mode process (understand → locate → read → update precisely) but both runs converged to the same result.
